text.title: Automated Persona Pipeline Architecture
text.summary: End-to-end automated persona generation for large datasets: ingestion, clustering, validation, narrative generation, and drift detection with schema stability requirements.

section.title: Pipeline Architecture
section.p: Four stages: Haiku ingests behavioral event exports via API and normalizes to feature matrix; Opus validates cluster separability; human approval gate; Sonnet transforms validated clusters into persona JSON documents consumable by downstream agents.

rule.statement: Store the persona JSON schema in version control; downstream agents depend on a stable schema and schema changes break them silently.
rule.rationale: Downstream agents (copywriting, feature-priority, pricing) consume persona data as structured input. Unversioned schema changes break their prompts without surfacing an error.

rule.statement: Add schema validation at the pipeline entry point (analytics export ingestion); schema changes in Amplitude, Mixpanel, or Heap exports silently break normalization.
rule.rationale: Analytics platforms change their export format without notice. Validation at ingestion surfaces the break immediately rather than propagating corrupted data through clustering and narrative stages.

rule.statement: Set a persona refresh cadence (monthly or quarterly) and automate drift detection by comparing new cluster centroids against stored ones; flag centroid shift over 15% for human review.
rule.rationale: User behavior drifts with product changes and market shifts. Automated drift detection prevents stale personas from accumulating in downstream systems undetected.

rule.statement: Keep a persona changelog with date, data snapshot version, k, silhouette score, and a one-line summary of what changed each cycle.
rule.rationale: Without a changelog, persona evolution is invisible. Auditors and stakeholders cannot determine when a persona changed or why, making trust impossible to maintain.

rule.statement: Human approval gate between Opus (cluster validation) and Sonnet (narrative generation) is non-negotiable even in fully automated pipelines.
rule.rationale: A bad cluster produces a confidently-written persona that enters downstream systems without scrutiny. The gate is the single control that prevents confident-wrong output from propagating.

list.item: Scale: automated pipeline targets 1,000+ users where manual cluster review is infeasible
Schema: outputs must conform to a stable JSON schema for downstream agent consumption
Drift detection: recurring pipeline includes centroid comparison against stored baseline
Risk: removing human review from any stage produces confident wrong personas — the gate must be enforced even at scale

text.reference: Silhouette scoring across k range with weak-cluster abort
